core_tier_summary.derivation: Deterministic, precedence-ordered: (1) semantic_role Sugar -> sugar; (2) semantic_role Exploratory -> material (child-runtime is outside the Core contract, algebra.handle.domain); (3) kind moduleword -> material (Hosted Modules, SPECIFICATION.html section 9.3); (4) otherwise the tier of the entry home algebraic_family, where observation splits to material iff derived_from includes algebra.observation.digest or algebra.handle.domain, else identity. derived_from is NOT used as the primary signal because it lists every law a word obeys (e.g. arithmetic obeys Bubble pass-through), not what the word is. See docs/dev/ajisai-minimal-core-identity.md.

tiers.identity: Value-space separation and the three propagation disciplines (Bubble/NIL, Kleene K3 U, structured diagnostic). Part of Minimal Core.
tiers.flow: State-transformer monoid, modifiers, dictionary resolution. Part of Minimal Core.
tiers.material: Algorithms and hosted layers (exact arithmetic, vectors/tensors, hosted modules and effects, child runtime). Derived library / self-host targets; bound by identity laws but outside Minimal Core.
tiers.sugar: Sealed surface grammar (delimiters, symbol sugar, directives). Fixed frame, not a self-host target.

algebra_primitives.description: Kleene three-valued truth domain {T,F,U}; a direct-sum component of V, disjoint from numbers.
algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared k3-truth schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

algebra_primitives.description: Strong Kleene meet (greatest lower bound) on K3; the algebraic primitive AND derives from.
algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared k3-truth schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

algebra_primitives.description: Strong Kleene join (least upper bound) on K3; the algebraic primitive OR derives from.
algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared k3-truth schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

algebra_primitives.description: Order-reversing involution on K3 fixing U; the algebraic primitive NOT derives from.
algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared k3-truth schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

algebra_primitives.description: Exact binary field arithmetic over the numeric cost tiers (Tier 0 rationals, Tier 1 algebraic normal form), with cheapest-tier demotion; ADD/SUB/MUL/DIV are instances. Historical id: the retired Gosper bihomographic schema.
algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared exact-arithmetic schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

algebra_primitives.admission_reason: Accepted as the shared exact-arithmetic schema required by existing formalization entries; it is not represented as surface sugar or a hosted effect boundary.

entries.notes: Boolean value; distinct from numeric 1/1.
Boolean value; distinct from numeric 0/1.
Primitive operational absence; distinct from FALSE, numeric zero, and logical UNKNOWN. Reasoned NIL/Bubble values propagate through NIL-passthrough words.
Operational-absence predicate; retains the inspected value and projects absence into the K3 truth domain. Reports FALSE for the logical UNKNOWN (§2.3 firewall).
Reads the direct reason of an operational NIL as a lowerCamelCase protocol-string Text; retains the inspected value; NIL when there is no reason.
Reads the required origin protocol string of an operational NIL as Text; retains the inspected value.
Reads the required four-valued recoverability protocol string of an operational NIL as Text (not a two-valued boolean, per §4.5.0); retains the inspected value.
Reads the optional three-layer diagnosis of an operational NIL as a Record whose fields are §4.5.0 protocol strings; retains the inspected value; NIL when no diagnosis is attached.
Strong Kleene conjunction over TRUE/FALSE/UNKNOWN. The U rows require a Tier 2 operand no current word constructs; they are pinned by native logic tests.
Strong Kleene disjunction.
Strong Kleene negation.
Exact rational arithmetic; division by zero observes NIL rather than host exceptions.
Exact-real addition as a bihomographic arithmetic word; NIL operands bubble through unchanged.
Exact-real subtraction as a bihomographic arithmetic word; NIL operands bubble through unchanged.
Exact-real multiplication as a bihomographic arithmetic word; includes arbitrary-precision integer observations.
Exact-real division as a bihomographic arithmetic word; division by zero projects to NIL rather than a host exception.
Modulo is derived as x - floor(x/y)·y over exact reals, so it depends on integer projection plus exact arithmetic and preserves Bubble/NIL absence.
FLOOR — exact-real integer projection toward negative infinity. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.
CEIL — exact-real integer projection toward positive infinity. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.
ROUND — exact-real nearest-integer projection without float round trips. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.

QUANTIZE — banker's-rounding (ties-to-even) quantization of an exact value to a positive rational grid, emitting the exact residual r so that q + r = x with no loss (canonical SPECIFICATION.html §7.13; design docs/dev/fintech-value-integrity-design.md). FLOOR/CEIL/ROUND are its step=1 special cases. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations (an irrational operand projects to an Undecidable bubble). Status Sketched pending a dedicated entry in the mathematical formalization doc.

QUANTIZE-HALF-AWAY — QUANTIZE-family grid quantization rounding to nearest with ties away from zero (the grid generalisation of ROUND), emitting the exact residual so q + r = x. Projecting; irrational operands project to an Undecidable bubble. Status Sketched pending a mathematical-formalization entry.

QUANTIZE-FLOOR — QUANTIZE-family grid quantization toward negative infinity (the grid generalisation of FLOOR), emitting the exact residual so q + r = x. Projecting; irrational operands project to an Undecidable bubble. Status Sketched pending a mathematical-formalization entry.

QUANTIZE-CEIL — QUANTIZE-family grid quantization toward positive infinity (the grid generalisation of CEIL), emitting the exact residual so q + r = x. Projecting; irrational operands project to an Undecidable bubble. Status Sketched pending a mathematical-formalization entry.

QUANTIZE-TRUNC — QUANTIZE-family grid quantization toward zero (truncation), emitting the exact residual so q + r = x. Distinct from QUANTIZE-FLOOR on negative values. Projecting; irrational operands project to an Undecidable bubble. Status Sketched pending a mathematical-formalization entry.

CONSERVE — value-conservation guard: asserts the exact sum of a scalar parts vector equals a declared total, passing the parts through iff the equality is proven and failing loudly (channel error) on violation or an undecidable comparison (canonical SPECIFICATION.html §13.3; design docs/dev/fintech-value-integrity-design.md). The value-mass complement to the static flow-mass conservation of §13.1, and the enforcement half of penny-perfect allocation alongside QUANTIZE's residual. Rejects NIL operands. Status Sketched pending a mathematical-formalization entry.
Decided comparisons produce Boolean observations; budgeted equality can produce UNKNOWN.
Budgeted exact-real equality projected into the K3 truth domain; NIL operands pass through unchanged.
Inequality is the K3-domain complement of budgeted equality when decided, with UNKNOWN and NIL preserved by projection/passthrough.
Budgeted exact-real strict less-than projected into the K3 truth domain; undecidable comparisons yield UNKNOWN.
Less-than-or-equal is derived from the same budgeted order observation as LT/EQ, projected into K3 truth.
Greater-than is the operand-dual of LT over the shared budgeted-order primitive.
Greater-than-or-equal is the operand-dual of LTE over the shared budgeted-order primitive.
Surface exposure of the explicit budgeted-order primitive: returns -1/0/1 for decided order or UNKNOWN with agreedPrefix on budget exhaustion.
Irrational observations are canonical continued fractions, never sqrt(...) Debug-style wrappers or ~ rational approximations.
Consumption modifier that preserves operands and pushes the result.
Region modifier selecting the whole operand stack; conformance guards the previous underflow regression.
Top-of-stack region modifier; selects the singleton top region for the next state-transformer word.
Operand-consuming modifier; selects the consume/eat policy for the next state-transformer word.
No-op pipeline marker; observationally the identity state transformer.
Bubble/NIL coalescing handler; replaces operational absence with a fallback and keeps present values.
Identity control word over the interpreter state transformer.
K3-honest guarded state-transformer choice; UNKNOWN guards do not fire.
Executes a code value by reflecting it into state-transformer composition.
Parses source text and reflects the resulting program into state-transformer composition.
Functorial/applicative lift of a block over an indexed sequence.
Predicate restriction over an indexed sequence using a K3 truth-valued block.
Catamorphism over an indexed sequence with an explicit accumulator and combiner state transformer.
Anamorphism that generates an indexed sequence by repeatedly applying a state transition.
Existential predicate fold over an indexed sequence into the K3 truth domain.
Universal predicate fold over an indexed sequence into the K3 truth domain.
Counting fold over the predicate-satisfying elements of an indexed sequence.
entries.implementation_schema: predicate_fold(count) via traversal_scheme + exact count projection

entries.notes: Structural vector and tensor observations, including shape/rank and broadcast arithmetic.
Indexed projection over vectors/tensors; out-of-range indices project to NIL/Bubble while preserving the source vector observation.
Point insertion as an ordered-sequence transform over the vector index structure.
Point replacement as an ordered-sequence transform over the vector index structure.
Point removal as an ordered-sequence transform; removing the last element projects to NIL/Bubble.
Cardinality observation over the indexed-sequence structure.
Prefix/suffix projection over the indexed-sequence structure.
Chunking an indexed sequence into ordered subsequences.
Free-monoid concatenation of ordered vector sequences.
Involutive order reversal on indexed sequences.
Deterministic construction of an inclusive integer indexed sequence.
Permutation action over an indexed vector sequence.
Stack-to-vector collection that constructs an indexed sequence from the top N stack values.
Shape observation for a tensor/indexed sequence.
Rank observation: the dimension count of a tensor/indexed sequence.
Reshape-group action that changes tensor shape without changing element order or count.
Axis-swap action in the tensor reshape group; 2-D transpose is an involution.
Tensor constructor that fills a requested shape with a repeated value.
Record laws exist, but conformance still needs representative record observations before marking Formalized here.
String algebra has law coverage; conformance still needs stable encoding/role cases.
Display-role retagging into canonical continued-fraction serialization; it preserves the underlying exact value.
Splits a text value into the finite codepoint sequence of one-character text values.
Folds a vector of text/codepoint fragments back into one text value.
Whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences; idempotent at fixed points.
Left-sided whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences.
Right-sided whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences.
Splits a codepoint sequence by a separator sequence into ordered text parts.
Codepoint-sequence replacement transform over all occurrences of a substring.
Prefix predicate over codepoint sequences, projected into the K3 truth domain.
Suffix predicate over codepoint sequences, projected into the K3 truth domain.
Parses text/codepoint sequences into exact numbers; malformed numeric text projects to NIL/Bubble.
Renders a value into canonical text/codepoint-sequence form under the text role.
Parses or projects a value into the K3 truth domain.
Maps an integer codepoint into a one-character text value; invalid codepoints project to NIL/Bubble.
User dictionaries and module imports are deterministic name-resolution observations.
PRINT is the outbound rendering boundary: it renders the consumed value and appends a host effect after capability gating.

entries.notes: Definition-time staging composes the block transformer into dictionary installation while preserving the resulting user-word observation.
DEL removes user dictionary bindings; for fresh definitions, DEF followed by DEL restores name resolution to Unknown, while module dictionaries remain intact.
LOOKUP observes the active dictionary/name-resolution state and emits structured documentation/diagnostics without changing the dictionary.
FORC is the dependency-graph override modifier consumed by protected destructive dictionary transitions such as redefining or deleting referenced user words.
IMPORT-ONLY is the selective visibility operator on module dictionaries: it exposes exactly selected Module Words and treats Core-listed boundary selectors as no-ops.
UNIMPORT shrinks a module visibility set while preserving referenced module words, acting as the inverse of full import when no references pin the module.
UNIMPORT-ONLY hides selected Module Words and rejects selectors pinned by user-word dependencies.
